$comment: Vendor registry — the root resources discovery walks. `npm run wings:discover` reads each source's listUrls, pulls the shop's product feed (WooCommerce Store API / Shopify products.json / HTML for Zoho), and writes anything not already in kits.json to candidates.json for review. `npm run wings:refresh` re-checks price/stock for indexed kits. Platform + quirks below were established by probing each site live — don't 'tidy' them without re-probing.

sources.name: Vortex-RC
sources.notes: The backbone of the Indian wing market — designs and makes EPP wings domestically. There is NO flying-wings category: the wings sit inside rc-plane-kits (51), tuff-birds-planes (14) and laser-cut-rc-airplane-kits-india (13), so discovery pulls planes+trainers too and triage has to sort them. Product pages: read the `data-product_variations` JSON for per-variant price + is_in_stock. There are NO kit/PNP labels; the axes are Kit Options x Electronics x Center Bay x Colour, so a flat kit/pnp field cannot represent them. Price is a RANGE and the top of the range is often the out-of-stock variant — never show the range max as 'the price'.

sources.name: UAV Marketplace
sources.notes: Clean schema.org Product JSON-LD with price + availability. Stocks LDARC / Skywalker / HEEWING / MAPBIRD / ATOMRC. Deep MRP strike-throughs (e.g. 6,999 against 17,899 MRP) — store both, and don't trust MRP as a real reference price. Root links: the /category/fixed-wing path is a 404 — the real listings live under /collections/<slug>/<id>.

sources.name: RoboSync Kits
sources.taxNote: Prices are shown ex-GST with '+18% GST' — multiply by 1.18 before display or every total lies.
sources.notes: The ONLY domestic source of a ZOHD airframe (Nano Talon EVO). Fixed-wing shelf is ATOMRC / Mapbird / XUAV / TBS. Mostly V-tail pushers, so most of it lands outside a wings-only scope — relevant when scope widens. Writes PROTOCOL-RELATIVE hrefs (//robosynckits.in/product/...), so the crawler must resolve hrefs against the page URL rather than regex absolute URLs.

sources.name: Ubuy (grey import)
sources.notes: NOT a stockist — a US drop-shipper. Its own page text: '*All items will import from US', 'the recipient is the importer of record', warranty may be void. Listed price EXCLUDES shipping + customs, so it is not a landed price; assume roughly +40-70%. Suppresses price entirely on out-of-stock items (the price-box never resolves past its skeleton). Only ever surface these behind an explicit import warning.

sources.name: Desertcart
sources.notes: DO NOT LINK. Its prices are scraped placeholder junk — it advertised a ZOHD Nano Talon EVO at INR 4,893 'customs included' when the bare plane costs $139.99 internationally. Delivery estimates are site-wide FAQ boilerplate, not real. Verified 2026-07-17.

sources.name: Robu.in
sources.notes: Sits behind a Cloudflare challenge — cannot be enumerated programmatically. Components house rather than an airframe shop; no wing listing surfaced via domain-scoped search. Treat as manual-check-only; absence is probable, not confirmed.

sources.name: IndiaMART
sources.notes: B2B lead-gen: listings show 'Contact Supplier' and publish NO price (no schema.org offer node). Link only — never attach a price. Stock text is seller-self-reported.

sources.name: Amazon.in
sources.notes: Do not scrape — use the Product Advertising API once enrolled in Associates. Gotcha: Amazon SUPPRESSES the price on unavailable ASINs (renders outOfStockBuyBox with no corePrice), and any rupee figures left in the HTML belong to sponsored carousels, not the product. Also seen: /dp/<asin> silently serving a different ASIN.

sources.name: Anubis RC
sources.notes: Owner-supplied root. Category HTML is JS-rendered — zero product links in the markup — so HTML scraping finds nothing. Use the WooCommerce Store API (/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products?category=<id>), which works and returns price + stock.

sources.name: DRK Store
sources.notes: Owner-supplied root. The category is named 'RTF/PNP QUADS' but it DOES carry fixed wings — only one today, and the owner reports they add more over time, so keep crawling it and let triage find them. Non-standard permalinks (products sit at /<slug>/, not /product/<slug>/) and JS-rendered listings, so only the WooCommerce Store API works.

sources.name: TujoRC
sources.notes: Owner-supplied root. Standard WooCommerce; stocks Durafly/FMS-class PNF airframes.

sources.name: Aeromodelling Tutor
sources.notes: Owner-supplied root. Shopify — /collections/<handle>/products.json returns everything with prices, no HTML parsing. Intermittently answers 503 on the first hit; retry (the job does).
